svg.text: How selection moves a value in a self-training loop
its scores rise with the value
It keeps the ones it scores highest
the kept answers' mean minus the
self-only pool: own mean = whole pool mean, so training displacement = selector gap · repeat about 4 rounds
The size of that selector gap is set by two dials — and here is how each behaves over a run:
times how well the judge sorts

Unit-form forecast, no fitted coefficient: selector gap ≈ agreement × spread fits the 290 agreement-scored rounds at R² 0.810, mean absolute error
0.042 in value units; agreement × spread tracks the selector gap at r = 0.90.
